description: On a long-running or never-exiting Bash command, encourage background execution and token-efficient polling. Matches on tool_input.command, so it is silent for plan-mode entry and every non-Bash tool (which carry no command). Self-cancelling for short-lived commands.

text: This looks like a long-running or never-exiting process (dev server, watcher, tail). Run it in the background rather than blocking the turn.

Design for token efficiency: when you poll it, surface only the output that matters - the error, the 'ready' line, the specific signal you are waiting for. Filter at the source (grep, tail, --quiet) instead of piping whole logs into context.
If this command is short-lived, ignore this guidance.
